REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501,DEFENDANT ANDREW MCCORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. DEMURRER TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING IS AVAILABLE IN LANDLORD-TENANT CASES. DEMURRER TO THE TWELVES CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE SPECIFIC MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE WITH KNOWLEDGE OF FALSITY AND INTENT TO DEFRAUD. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE SAMUEL K. FENG, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Andrew Mccord To Strike Allegations Of Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Mahfam Sadeghi In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Megan Johnson, Esq., 415-969-6973, appearing for Bethany Mongeau, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Mahfam Sadeghi, Esq., 626-921-4300, appearing for Andrew McCord, for Defendant and moving party.

Argued. Ms. Johnson stated that she contested to the tentative. Ms. Sadeghi argued that plaintiff repeats the same set of facts.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW MCCORD Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Andrew Mccord To Strike Allegations Of Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs Complaint is DENIED. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to show reckless disregard of tenant's rights. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Megan Johnson, Esq., 415-969-6973, appearing for Bethany Mongeau, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Mahfam Sadeghi, Esq., 626-921-4300, appearing for Andrew McCord, for Defendant and moving party.

Argued. Ms. Johnson stated that she contested to the tentative. Ms. Sadeghi argued that plaintiff repeats the same set of facts.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW MCCORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the ninth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that the cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing is available in landlord-tenant cases. Demurrer to the twelves cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege specific misrepresentations made with knowledge of falsity and intent to defraud. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Continued To Mar-11-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Continued To Feb-13-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW MCCORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to February 13, 2020. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than January 24, 2020 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than January 24, 2020 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date.
